Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

An elastomeric bearing pad consisting of two steel plates bounded to a chlorophene elastomer, is subjected to a shear force V during a static loading test as shown in figure. The pad has dimensions a = b = 200 mm, and the elastomer has thickness t = 40 mm. When the force F equals 20 kN, the top plate is found to have displaced laterally 10.00 mm with respect to the bottom
plate. The shear modulus of elasticity G of the chlorophene is ______ MPa.

 
NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2 (2 - 2)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Two solid cylindrical rods AB and BC are welded together at B and loaded as shown in figure.
If the average normal stress must not exceed 200 MPa in either rod, the allowable values of the
diameter d1 and d2 are

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A steel bar 0.5 m long and cross-section of 10 mm × 50 mm is compressed under an axial load of 100 kN applied in the direction of its length. If net strain in one direction is prevented by applying a suitable compressive load, then the absolute change in length will be _____ mm.
[Take E = 210 GPa and μ = 0.3] (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.43
Solution

0.43 (0.40 - 0.45)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Two solid members are joined together using a 60° scarf weld AB as shown in figure. The average normal and average shear stress resisted in the plane of the weld are

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The rigid beam AC shown in figure, is supported by a pin at A and wires BD and CE. If the load P on the beam causes the end C to be displaced 20 mm downward, the normal strain developed in wires BD are

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In the figure shown, a bar AB having length L and axial rigidity EA is fixed at end A. At the other end a small gap of dimension x exists between the end of the bar and a rigid surface. A load P acts on the bar point C, which is  of the length from the fixed end. If the support reactions produced by the load P are to be equal in magnitude, then size x of the gap will be

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

For the axial loading shown in figure, the loading is hydrostatic with σx = σy = σz = –100 MPa.
The change in volume of the brass cylinder is ______ mm3. E = 200 GPa, μ = 0.33

 
NAT
Correct answer: -459
Solution

-459 (-460 - -457)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The column shown in figure, is constructed from high-strength concrete with EC = 30 GPa and 5 - A – 36 steel reinforcing rods with Esteel = 200 GPa. It is subjected to an axial force of 500 kN. If one-fifth of the load is carried by the steel and fourth-fifth by the concrete, diameter of each
rod will be ______ mm. [Assume five steel rod inserted for given structure area]

 
NAT
Correct answer: 23.98
Solution

23.98 (22 - 25)

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q13 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In the figure shown, a simple beam with a uniform load is pin supported at one end and spring supported at the other. The spring has stiffness k = 42 which of the following statements are correct?

 
 
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)

 
 
 
 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A steel bar, 300 mm long and 20 mm × 20 mm cross-section, is subjected to a tensile force of 200 kN in the direction of its length. The change in volume will be _____ mm3, if E = 2 × 105 N/mm2 and μ = 1/3.

NAT
Correct answer: 100
Solution

100 (100 - 101)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A laminated wooden beam 100 mm wide and 150 mm deep is made of three 100 mm × 50 mm planks glued together to resist longitudinal shear. The beam is simply supported over a span of 3 m. If the allowable shearing stress in the glued joint is 0.6 N/mm2, the safe UDL the beam can
carry will be _____ N/m.

 
NAT
Correct answer: 4500
Solution

4500 (4490 - 4520)
